page: Amazon Web Services m8ib.96xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with dedicated Intel processors running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system provides 384 virtual CPUs mapped to 192 physical cores, utilizing a configuration of 2.0 threads per core.

The memory configuration consists of 1536.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced resource density of 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8ib.96xlarge offers a high-density allocation of dedicated compute resources and memory capacity within a single virtualized environment. This makes it suitable for large-scale enterprise workloads, high-concurrency web serving, and distributed applications that require substantial memory and processing capabilities without the need for local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.96xlarge is a general-purpose server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor and powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It delivers 384 dedicated vCPUs across 192 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps but does not include local storage or GPU hardware. This configuration is designed for large-scale enterprise applications, high-concurrency web serving, and memory-intensive general-purpose workloads that require high-throughput networking and dedicated compute resources without local disk requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.96xlarge is a general-purpose Nitro instance featuring 384 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 1536.0 GB of RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for large-scale enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel processors, massive memory allocation, and high-speed baseline networking.
